title: AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate
description: Scenario practice batch for Design High-Performing Architectures.

questions.question: Users around the world download static assets from an S3 bucket, but latency is high in distant Regions. Which change will improve performance the most?
answers: Move the bucket to a public subnet
Use Amazon CloudFront in front of S3
Store the files on Amazon EBS
questions.shortExplanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations and is the standard way to reduce latency for globally distributed users.
questions.fullExplanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations and is the standard way to reduce latency for globally distributed users.
questions.explanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations and is the standard way to reduce latency for globally distributed users.

questions.question: A DynamoDB table experiences uneven traffic and throttling on a small set of partition keys. What should the architect review first?
questions.shortExplanation: Hot partitions are usually a partition key design problem rather than a network problem.
questions.fullExplanation: Hot partitions are usually a partition key design problem rather than a network problem.
questions.explanation: Hot partitions are usually a partition key design problem rather than a network problem.

questions.question: An API reads the same product catalog data repeatedly and the database is becoming a bottleneck. Which option should be added?
questions.shortExplanation: ElastiCache is the managed caching layer intended to offload repeated reads from the primary database.
questions.fullExplanation: ElastiCache is the managed caching layer intended to offload repeated reads from the primary database.
questions.explanation: ElastiCache is the managed caching layer intended to offload repeated reads from the primary database.

questions.question: A team is lifting a CPU-intensive application to AWS. Which EC2 family should be evaluated first?
questions.shortExplanation: CPU-bound workloads usually start with compute optimized families because they match the bottleneck profile.
questions.fullExplanation: CPU-bound workloads usually start with compute optimized families because they match the bottleneck profile.
questions.explanation: CPU-bound workloads usually start with compute optimized families because they match the bottleneck profile.

questions.question: A media processing pipeline can run many tasks independently. Which design improves throughput?
answers: Serialize every task on one instance
Parallelize the jobs across multiple workers
Move the files to a public S3 bucket
Reduce the number of metrics collected
questions.shortExplanation: Independent work should be parallelized to increase throughput and shorten total processing time.
questions.fullExplanation: Independent work should be parallelized to increase throughput and shorten total processing time.
questions.explanation: Independent work should be parallelized to increase throughput and shorten total processing time.

questions.question: An application experiences slow startup on every Lambda invocation because it recreates clients for downstream services. What should be changed?
answers: Create clients outside the handler for reuse
Move the function into a public subnet
questions.shortExplanation: Reusing clients outside the handler is a common Lambda optimization that cuts repeated setup latency.
questions.fullExplanation: Reusing clients outside the handler is a common Lambda optimization that cuts repeated setup latency.
questions.explanation: Reusing clients outside the handler is a common Lambda optimization that cuts repeated setup latency.

questions.question: A workload needs high throughput block storage for a database on EC2. Which option should be considered?
answers: General Purpose SSD or Provisioned IOPS EBS volumes
questions.shortExplanation: Database performance on EC2 depends heavily on choosing an EBS volume type that fits the required IOPS and throughput.
questions.fullExplanation: Database performance on EC2 depends heavily on choosing an EBS volume type that fits the required IOPS and throughput.
questions.explanation: Database performance on EC2 depends heavily on choosing an EBS volume type that fits the required IOPS and throughput.

questions.question: A user-facing request triggers a long image rendering task. What architectural change most improves response time?
answers: Do all rendering synchronously before replying
Move the render into an asynchronous workflow
Lower the EC2 security group limits
questions.shortExplanation: Asynchronous processing removes slow operations from the request path and improves user-perceived performance.
questions.fullExplanation: Asynchronous processing removes slow operations from the request path and improves user-perceived performance.
questions.explanation: Asynchronous processing removes slow operations from the request path and improves user-perceived performance.

questions.question: A team wants to understand which service call contributes most to end-user latency before optimizing. What should be added?
questions.shortExplanation: Tracing and metrics expose where the time is actually being spent so tuning effort stays evidence-based.
questions.fullExplanation: Tracing and metrics expose where the time is actually being spent so tuning effort stays evidence-based.
questions.explanation: Tracing and metrics expose where the time is actually being spent so tuning effort stays evidence-based.

questions.question: A workload receives unpredictable bursts of event traffic and needs to scale quickly without provisioning idle servers. Which option fits best?
answers: One large EC2 instance with cron jobs
A fixed-size Auto Scaling group of one
questions.shortExplanation: Event-driven Lambda is well suited for bursty traffic because it scales on demand without idle server cost.
questions.fullExplanation: Event-driven Lambda is well suited for bursty traffic because it scales on demand without idle server cost.
questions.explanation: Event-driven Lambda is well suited for bursty traffic because it scales on demand without idle server cost.

questions.question: A service sends large files to users repeatedly and the same objects are requested many times per minute. Which design best reduces origin load?
questions.shortExplanation: Edge caching reduces repeated origin reads and lowers latency for popular content.
questions.fullExplanation: Edge caching reduces repeated origin reads and lowers latency for popular content.
questions.explanation: Edge caching reduces repeated origin reads and lowers latency for popular content.

questions.question: A DynamoDB workload has acceptable average latency but periodic throttling under uneven access patterns. Which exam trap best explains this?
answers: The table has too many GSIs
The workload likely has a hot partition
The VPC needs another NAT gateway
The IAM role is too permissive
questions.shortExplanation: Even with enough total capacity, hot partitions can cause throttling and uneven latency.
questions.fullExplanation: Even with enough total capacity, hot partitions can cause throttling and uneven latency.
questions.explanation: Even with enough total capacity, hot partitions can cause throttling and uneven latency.

questions.question: A web application compresses every payload aggressively and response times worsen under load. What tradeoff is most likely causing the issue?
answers: Compression saves CPU but increases latency
Compression reduces transfer but increases CPU cost
Compression forces traffic through one AZ
questions.shortExplanation: Compression can lower transfer size but may become a CPU bottleneck under high concurrency.
questions.fullExplanation: Compression can lower transfer size but may become a CPU bottleneck under high concurrency.
questions.explanation: Compression can lower transfer size but may become a CPU bottleneck under high concurrency.

questions.question: A relational database was moved to very fast compute instances but query latency remains poor. Which additional area should the architect inspect?
questions.shortExplanation: Performance bottlenecks often persist when compute is upgraded but storage configuration or query patterns remain weak.
questions.fullExplanation: Performance bottlenecks often persist when compute is upgraded but storage configuration or query patterns remain weak.
questions.explanation: Performance bottlenecks often persist when compute is upgraded but storage configuration or query patterns remain weak.

questions.question: A latency-sensitive microservice performs many small synchronous calls to another service in the same workflow. Which redesign most likely improves performance?
answers: Batch or asynchronous interaction where possible
questions.shortExplanation: Reducing chattiness through batching or asynchronous interaction is a classic performance optimization for service-to-service latency.
questions.fullExplanation: Reducing chattiness through batching or asynchronous interaction is a classic performance optimization for service-to-service latency.
questions.explanation: Reducing chattiness through batching or asynchronous interaction is a classic performance optimization for service-to-service latency.
